title: Signal Dry Bulk Weekly Report
summary:
Chart of the Week : Dry Bulk Soyabeans Flow from Brazil to China
Higher monthly Brazilian soybean volumes to China for 2023
During the second week of November, freight rates showed a weaker outlook due to the lack of adequate support from demand growth rates. Additionally, the number of ballasters for Capesize vessels in Southeast Africa has notably increased over the past three weeks, further fueled by uncertainty surrounding Chinese economic activity. However, there is a glimmer of hope for the market as iron ore prices have recently been boosted by Beijing's efforts to stimulate and stabilise its economy.

children.title: Chart of the Week : Dry Bulk Soyabeans Flow from Brazil to China
section_path: Chart of the Week : Dry Bulk Soyabeans Flow from Brazil to China
children.section_path_text: Chart of the Week : Dry Bulk Soyabeans Flow from Brazil to China
children.summary:
Higher monthly Brazilian soybean volumes to China for 2023
Image reference: unnamed - 2023-11-08T103643.288.png | assets/2023-11-08_signal-dry-bulk-weekly-report_img_unnamed-2023-11-08t103643288_e5e04edc65d5.png
During the second week of November, freight rates showed a weaker outlook due to the lack of adequate support from demand growth rates. Additionally, the number of ballasters for Capesize vessels in Southeast Africa has notably increased over the past three weeks, further fueled by uncertainty surrounding Chinese economic activity.

children.title: SECTION 1/ FREIGHT
children.summary:
‘The Big Picture' - Capesize and Panamax Bulkers and Smaller Ship Sizes Market Rates ($/t) Weaker
Image reference: unnamed - 2023-11-08T103742.111.png | assets/2023-11-08_signal-dry-bulk-weekly-report_img_unnamed-2023-11-08t103742111_59b4954bbb37.png
The second week of November brings downward pressure on freight market sentiment, with the Capesize segment seeing a steep correction from the week 42 peak. Capesize vessel freight rates from Brazil to North China decreased by 20% to $18/tonne per month.

children.title: SECTION 2/ SUPPLY
children.summary:
Supply Trend Lines for Key Load Areas Ballasters (#vessels) Mixed
Image reference: unnamed - 2023-11-08T103941.685.png | assets/2023-11-08_signal-dry-bulk-weekly-report_img_unnamed-2023-11-08t103941685_60c835662fde.png
In the second week of November, there was a mixed picture for ballasters. Capesize and Handysize saw significant increases, while the other vessel size segments continued the decreasing trend from October.

children.title: SECTION 3/ DEMAND
children.summary:
Summary of Dry Bulk Demand, per Ship Size Tonne Days Decreasing
Image reference: unnamed - 2023-11-08T104059.707.png | assets/2023-11-08_signal-dry-bulk-weekly-report_img_unnamed-2023-11-08t104059707_89f9e04dbf00.png
Demand growth declined sharply across all vessel sizes in the past three weeks, with a particularly steep drop in Panamax and Handysize vessels. Capesize: The downward trend seems to have bottomed out and there are no signs of improvement.

children.title: SECTION 4/ PORT CONGESTION
children.summary:
Dry bulk ships congested at Chinese ports No of Vessels Increasing
Image reference: unnamed - 2023-11-08T104215.424.png | assets/2023-11-08_signal-dry-bulk-weekly-report_img_unnamed-2023-11-08t104215424_f619ebb2edf6.png
The level of ship congestion in the Panamax and Supramax segments increased, resulting in one of the highest weekly spikes since week 40. Capesize: The number of congested vessels rose to 129, 8 higher than the previous week.
